includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Contamination Removal Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive database of contamination removal parameters, safety data, and laser cleaning specifications

contaminant.name: Wax Coating Buildup
contaminant.description: Wax-buildup contamination arises from paraffin, carnauba, and synthetic deposits in protective coatings and polishes, and it accumulates layer by layer on surfaces. This buildup, hydrophobic and soft in texture, distinguishes itself from greasy oils or dust by even spreading and resistance to solvents. On metals such as steel and aluminum, it clings tightly yet vaporizes readily under laser pulse, thus challenges like incomplete removal diminish, and laser cleaning succeeds through precise ablation without substrate harm.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Formaldehyde, Acrolein, Benzene - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde, Benzene - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
